author: Dr. Lizard
title: What genes other than PIGA can cause paroxysmal nocturnal hemoglobinuria?
answer: Rare cases involve PIGM or PIGT mutations, but PIGA accounts for over 95 percent of PNH cases.
detail: PIGA encodes the first enzyme in GPI anchor biosynthesis, making it the most common mutation site.
PIGM and PIGT encode other enzymes in the GPI anchor synthesis pathway, causing similar phenotypes.
HMGA genes encode high mobility group proteins involved in chromatin remodeling and are unrelated to PNH pathogenesis.
All PIG gene mutations result in deficient GPI anchor expression, leading to loss of CD55 and CD59.
releaseNote: This card discusses the genetic causes of paroxysmal nocturnal hemoglobinuria (PNH), with a focus on genes other than PIGA.
